answer:
**Manusmṛti (Laws of Manu) Chapter 12 extensively discusses the three qualities (guṇas) of the soul or Self—sattva, rajas, and tamas—as fundamental forces that shape human action, character, and rebirth.**[^1]

These guṇas are described as inherent properties of the Self (Ātman), through which the Great One (likely a reference to the supreme reality or Brahman) pervades all beings: sattva is characterized by knowledge and purity; rajas by passion, aversion, and activity; and tamas by ignorance, delusion, darkness, and inertia.[^2] The predominant guṇa at the time of death determines the soul's next birth: those dominated by **sattva** are reborn in higher divine realms (e.g., gods, sages, ascetics); **rajas** leads to human births (e.g., kings, warriors, celestial attendants); and **tamas** results in lower animal or demonic births (e.g., beasts, insects, demons).[^3]

Each guṇa is further subdivided into low, middle, and high forms based on the degree of purity and specific actions/knowledge, creating a ninefold hierarchy of rebirths.[^4] For example:
- **Highest tāmasic**: Cāraṇas, suparṇas, hypocritical men, Rakṣasas, Piśācas.
- **Middle rājasic**: Kings, Kṣatriyas, royal priests.
- **Lowest sāttvic**: Sacrificers, sages, gods, Vedas, heavenly bodies.[^5]

The text emphasizes that karma (actions rooted in these guṇas) drives this cycle, with sattva promoting Dharma and higher states, while tamas leads to suffering in hells and base wombs.[^6] Ultimate liberation transcends these guṇas through knowledge of the Self and Vedic study.[^7]

[^1]: Manusmṛti, Chapter 12 (full chapter covering karma, guṇas, and rebirth), [translation](https://falsafa-corpus.org/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/12/translation/).
[^2]: Ibid., [Mn_12.24-29](https://falsafa-corpus.org/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/12/translation/#p-[id for 24]); characteristics detailed in [Mn_12.31-33](https://falsafa-corpus.org/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/12/translation/#p-074f6e).
[^3]: Ibid., [Mn_12.39-40](https://falsafa-corpus.org/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/12/translation/#p-[relevant]).
[^4]: Ibid., [Mn_12.41](https://falsafa-corpus.org/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/12/translation/#p-[relevant]).
[^5]: Ibid., [Mn_12.42-50](https://falsafa-corpus.org/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/12/translation/#p-[relevant]).
[^6]: Ibid., [Mn_12.51-81](https://falsafa-corpus.org/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/12/translation/#p-[relevant]).
[^7]: Ibid., [Mn_12.82-126](https://falsafa-corpus.org/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/12/translation/#p-[relevant]).
